description: The purpose of a tool, action, or arrangement is what it is for. With 'porua', that intended use or end can be stated before the result exists. 'thueli' (intend) concerns an agent's present aim, while cause and consequence describe what actually brought an event about and what followed.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into rounded middle-height back 'o'. A tap or short trill forms 'r' before stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Lower the jaw and unround into a separate final 'a' across the 'u.a' hiatus.
usage_notes: The person, object, or activity whose purpose is meant precedes the noun, as in 'keli porua' (device purpose). A purpose may be personal, shared, assigned, contested, or discovered in use. Fulfilling it still requires a stated criterion.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Attending to purpose makes the intended direction of an act available before habit carries it forward. The later effect can then be examined without asking good intent to stand in for it.

semantic_domains.activity: the end or use towards which an action or arrangement is directed
semantic_domains.cognition: an intended use or end held in thought before or during action
